mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or positive returns occur in less than 50% of rate-cut months.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.5%, labor market deterioration triggers recession expectations that compress equity valuations and produce negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in 65% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.5%, or the signal loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and industrial production confirms broad-based demand strength, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns as earnings growth expectations rise.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a single-proxy baseline, or interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant in multivariate regression over 5 years.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation forces markets to price in aggressive central bank tightening, causing immediate negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in over 60% of high-inflation months, or the inflation-return correlation flips positive during the specified regime.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which directly supports corporate earnings and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The signal produces a negative rank IC over consecutive 2-year windows or fails to predict return direction in more than 45% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 1.1 million annualized units signals collapsing construction demand and weak consumer confidence, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Market returns remain positive in 70% of months where housing starts breach the threshold, or the predictive power vanishes after controlling for interest rates.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total nonfarm payrolls and falling unemployment rates indicates robust labor absorption and wage growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns through consumption channel.
failure_condition: The interaction effect yields no incremental predictive power over single-series models, or the joint condition fails to separate winning and losing months at p < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth exceeding 2.5% confirms sustained economic expansion, reinforcing equity risk premiums and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in 55% of quarters where GDP growth surpasses 2.5%, or the signal loses explanatory power when inflation exceeds 3%.
